title: valeriana dioica subsp. dioica
intro: Valeriana dioica subsp. dioica, also known as the blue valerian or valerian root, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Valerianaceae. It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia, known for its medicinal properties, particularly its sedative and anti-anxiety effects. The roots of the plant contain valerenic acid and valeranone, which contribute to its health benefits, including relieving insomnia, restlessness, and stress, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality. Valeriana dioica subsp. dioica can be prepared in various herbal forms, including dried root, tea, tincture, and capsules, which can be used to address a range of health issues.
